title: Viewing Insights and Grades in Teams
what_it_is: Insights is a built-in Teams tool that pulls together engagement and completion trends for your class into one dashboard — things like how many learners have handed in an Assignment, and how activity is spread across the group over time. Grades is a separate tab in your class team that lists every learner alongside their grade for each Assignment, all in one table.
why_use_it: Instead of opening each Assignment separately to check who's submitted or looking up grades one learner at a time, Insights and Grades bring that information together in one place, so you're not repeating the same steps for every learner.
how_to: Open your class team in Teams.
Select the Insights tab at the top of the team. If you can't see it, select the + to the right of your existing tabs and search for Insights to add it.
The Insights dashboard opens, showing engagement and completion trends for the group, such as Assignment completion rates and activity over time.
To see grades, select the Grades tab at the top of the team instead. If it isn't there, add it the same way you added Insights.
The Grades tab shows a table with every learner listed down the left and every Assignment across the top, with each grade filled in once you've returned it.
Select a column heading to sort the table by that Assignment, or select a row to see one learner's grades across everything they've submitted.
